message: fix: close NetworkX parity stubs (br-r37-c1-dwasp, br-r37-c1-7i2dj)

files.patch:
@@ -38,6 +38,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-71glx","title":"parity: 3 signature mismatches (gaussian_random_partition_graph arg order, find_minimal_d_separator wrong sig, to_nested_tuple leaks _parent)","description":"Three signature-parity defects discovered while signature-diffing fnx __all__ vs upstream nx (excluding backend dispatch noise). (1) gaussian_random_partition_graph: nx is (n, s, v, p_in, p_out, directed=False, seed=None) but fnx is (n, s, v, p_in, p_out, seed=None, directed=False) — positional arg order swapped, breaks calls like nx.gaussian_random_partition_graph(10, 5, 0.1, 0.4, 0.05, True). (2) find_minimal_d_separator: nx is (G, x, y, *, included=None, restricted=None) but fnx is (G, u, v) — wrong param names AND missing keyword-only included/restricted. (3) to_nested_tuple: fnx leaks an internal _parent=None recursion arg in its public signature. Fix: align signatures; for find_minimal_d_separator, route through nx via _call_networkx_for_parity to handle the new kwargs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:49:37.606667646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:38.730801947Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:38.728883324Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 21b0340: 3 signature fixes (gaussian_random_partition_graph arg order, find_minimal_d_separator delegated to nx with included/restricted kwargs, to_nested_tuple no longer leaks _parent). Smoke test still passes 170/170.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-75r94","title":"parity: coloring strategy_* (8) + matching_dict_to_set (1) wrappers","description":"Two clusters of nx utilities not surfaced. Coloring strategies (used as strategy= argument to greedy_color): strategy_largest_first(G, colors), strategy_random_sequential(G, colors, seed=None), strategy_smallest_last(G, colors), strategy_independent_set(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential_bfs(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential_dfs(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential(G, colors, traversal=bfs), strategy_saturation_largest_first(G, colors). Matching utility: matching_dict_to_set(matching) — converts a {node: partner} dict to a set of (u, v) frozensets. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:29:37.916763877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:44:39.656055329Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:44:39.653993535Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 7939284: 8 strategy_* coloring callables + matching_dict_to_set. Verified with nx.greedy_color(C6, strategy=fnx.strategy_largest_first) matching nx output. Follow-up: fnx.greedy_color does not accept callable strategies (only strings) — separate parity gap.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-792dv","title":"edge_connectivity/node_connectivity/has_eulerian_path drift on single-node-with-self-loop","description":"On a graph with exactly one node and a single self-loop edge, three connectivity-related functions diverge from nx:

  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1, 1)])
  >>> fnx.edge_connectivity(g)         # 0   (nx returns 2)
  >>> fnx.node_connectivity(g)         # 0   (nx returns 2)
  >>> fnx.has_eulerian_path(g)         # False (nx returns True)

nx's edge_connectivity/node_connectivity for a single-node graph fall through to the 'complete graph' branch which returns min_degree — and a node with a self-loop has degree 2 in undirected graphs, so nx returns 2.

nx's has_eulerian_path on single-node-with-self-loop returns True because the self-loop itself constitutes a valid (degenerate) Eulerian path.

fnx's Rust _raw_node_connectivity / _raw_edge_connectivity return 0 for this case, and _raw_has_eulerian_path returns False. All three are incorrect relative to nx.

Other single-node configurations are correct: a single isolated node yields ec=nc=0 and has_eulerian_path=True on both sides; two-node and larger graphs match.

Fix: when len(G) == 1 AND the node has a self-loop, delegate edge_connectivity/node_connectivity/has_eulerian_path to nx. The Rust fast path remains the default for all other shapes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:11:45.784349360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T06:19:19.651183801Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T06:19:19.648392086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-7i2dj","title":"parity: random_k_out_graph must require alpha","description":"Upstream NetworkX random_k_out_graph requires the alpha positional argument: nx.random_k_out_graph(3, 1, seed=1) raises TypeError("random_k_out_graph() missing 1 required positional argument: 'alpha'"). FrankenNetworkX currently defines random_k_out_graph(n, k, alpha=1, self_loops=True, seed=None), so fnx.random_k_out_graph(3, 1, seed=1) silently succeeds and returns a DiGraph. This is an observable public API contract drift. Fix by removing the default alpha value from python/franken_networkx/__init__.py and add a Python conformance test that compares the missing-alpha TypeError against the legacy NetworkX oracle while preserving the valid alpha call path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:41:36.805672416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:45:53.712038929Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:45:53.709720568Z","close_reason":"Fixed random_k_out_graph parity: alpha is required like NetworkX, valid calls route through the NetworkX oracle and rehydrate as fnx.MultiDiGraph, with seeded conformance coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-7u6up","title":"parity: community edge_betweenness_partition + greedy_source_expansion missing","description":"Two community-detection helpers from networkx.algorithms.community not exposed by franken_networkx: edge_betweenness_partition(G, number_of_sets, weight=None) and greedy_source_expansion(G, source, ...). Add Python wrappers delegating via _call_networkx_community_for_parity, export at top-level and add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T12:13:47.981273951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T13:05:50.852667948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T13:05:50.850778351Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4eb0d6d: edge_betweenness_partition + greedy_source_expansion verified on karate","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-7zrd7","title":"parity: geometric graph generators missing kwargs (pos_name, weight_name, metric, p, p_dist, weight)","description":"Four geometric-graph generators had stale signatures vs nx: soft_random_geometric_graph (missing p, pos_name); waxman_graph (metric, pos_name); geographical_threshold_graph (metric, p_dist, pos_name, weight_name); thresholded_random_geometric_graph (weight, p, pos_name, weight_name). Drop-in callers passing any of those kwargs hit TypeError. Aligned all 4 signatures to nx exactly and implemented the new behavior: Minkowski p-norm distances, custom metric callable, configurable pos/weight attribute names, p_dist scoring fn, weight as callable/dict/scalar.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:15:43.861443164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:16:32.381315774Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:16:32.379452394Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit a3feb2d. All 4 geometric generators now match nx signatures + honour p/metric/pos_name/weight_name/p_dist/weight kwargs with proper Minkowski p-norm distance helper. 22 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-8hshk","title":"equivalence_classes returns list instead of nx's set (and wrong iter order)","description":"fnx.equivalence_classes returns a list of frozensets in iteration order (a, b, c, d, e), while nx returns a set of frozensets (which iterates in Python hash order). The return type and iteration order differ.

Repro:
  nodes = ['a','b','c','d','e']; eq = lambda x,y: x == y
  fnx -> [frozenset({'a'}), frozenset({'b'}), ...] (list, input order)
  nx  -> {frozenset({'d'}), frozenset({'a'}), frozenset({'b'}), ...} (set, hash order)
  
  list(nx.equivalence_classes(...)) yields hash-order frozensets.
  list(fnx.equivalence_classes(...)) yields input-order frozensets.

Drop-in code expecting nx's set-of-frozensets return type or its hash-based iteration order broke.

Fix: delegate to nx (small function, simple delegation). Mirrors the cycle_basis / find_cliques / SCC delegation pattern.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:15:26.239493580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:17:56.538339706Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:17:56.536238627Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -65,6 +66,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-dc14n","title":"Graph.edges(nbunch) returns edges in wrong order vs nx","description":"fnx.Graph.edges(nbunch) (and .edges(node)) returns edges in a different iteration order than nx. The all-edges (no nbunch) case matches; only the filtered case drifts.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]  
  Both libs: adj['a'] = ['b','c']
  
  fnx.edges(['a','b']) -> [('b','c'), ('a','c'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges(['a','b']) -> [('a','b'), ('a','c'), ('b','c')]
  
  fnx.edges('a') -> [('a','c'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges('a') -> [('a','b'), ('a','c')]   (matches adj['a'] order)
  
  fnx.edges(['a','b','c']) -> [('c','d'), ('c','b'), ('c','a'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges(['a','b','c']) -> [('a','b'), ('a','c'), ('b','c'), ('c','d')]

nx's contract: iterate nbunch_list in user-given order, for each node yield edges (u, v) in adj[u] order, skipping edges where v is already-seen. fnx's Rust path appears to iterate G.nodes() (full graph) and filter by nbunch — completely different.

Drop-in code that iterated G.edges(nbunch) expecting nx's adj-ordered output broke. DiGraph cases (edges, in_edges, out_edges) match — only undirected edges() with nbunch drifts.

Fix: override EdgeDataView._materialize to iterate nbunch in user order with adj-iteration, mirroring nx's exact algorithm.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:42:44.701569209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:49:57.853658449Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:49:57.851586465Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-dg2dn","title":"core_number/k_core/k_shell/k_crust/k_corona accept self-loop graphs; nx raises NetworkXNotImplemented; eulerian_path drift on self-loops","description":"Two parity defects:

(1) core_number-family functions silently accept graphs with self-loops; nx raises NetworkXNotImplemented:

  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1,2),(2,3),(3,1),(1,1)])
  >>> fnx.core_number(g)        # silently returns {1: 2, 2: 2, 3: 2}
  >>> nx.core_number(nx.Graph(g))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: Input graph has self loops which is not permitted; Consider using G.remove_edges_from(nx.selfloop_edges(G)).

Affects core_number, k_core, k_shell, k_crust, k_corona — all share the _raw_core_number Rust binding which has no self-loop guard.

(2) eulerian_path raises 'Graph has no Eulerian paths' on graphs that ARE Eulerian (after the br-r37-c1-792dv has_eulerian_path/is_eulerian fix):

  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1,2),(2,3),(3,1),(1,1)])
  >>> fnx.has_eulerian_path(g)        # True (correct after br-r37-c1-792dv)
  >>> fnx.is_eulerian(g)               # True (correct)
  >>> list(fnx.eulerian_path(g))
  NetworkXError: Graph has no Eulerian paths.    # nx returns [(1,2),(2,3),(3,1),(1,1)]

The Rust _raw_eulerian_path mishandles self-loops the same way the corresponding boolean predicates did. Delegate to nx whenever the graph contains a self-loop.

Fix: add self-loop guard in core_number raising NetworkXNotImplemented with nx's wording. Add self-loop guard in eulerian_path delegating to nx.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:29:52.956609614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T06:36:03.395153162Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T06:36:03.392377045Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-dgicx","title":"florentine_families_graph node + edge + adj order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.florentine_families_graph builds the graph with edges in alphabetical/canonical order, but nx uses a specific narrative-order list of add_edge calls (Castellani's edges before Medici's middle edges, etc.). This produces different node-insertion order, edge iteration order, and per-node adj order.

Repro:
  fnx nodes: ['Acciaiuoli','Medici','Albizzi','Ginori','Guadagni','Barbadori','Castellani','Bischeri',...]
  nx  nodes: ['Acciaiuoli','Medici','Castellani','Peruzzi','Strozzi','Barbadori','Ridolfi','Tornabuoni',...]

  fnx adj['Medici']: ['Acciaiuoli','Albizzi','Barbadori','Ridolfi','Salviati','Tornabuoni']
  nx  adj['Medici']: ['Acciaiuoli','Barbadori','Ridolfi','Tornabuoni','Albizzi','Salviati']

Drop-in code that iterated this classic dataset's nodes/edges in nx order broke. Fix: replicate nx's exact add_edge sequence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:03:25.731453322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:05:45.516706801Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:05:45.514515137Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-dwasp","title":"Implement weighted all_shortest_paths for DiGraph","description":"mock-code-finder found a public stub: fnx.all_shortest_paths on DiGraph with weight set raises builtin NotImplementedError ('weighted all_shortest_paths is not yet supported for DiGraph' / bellman-ford variant), while upstream NetworkX returns all shortest directed weighted paths. Repro: DiGraph with edges 0->1, 0->2, 1->3, 2->3 all weight=1; list(fnx.all_shortest_paths(G, 0, 3, weight='weight')) raises, but nx returns [[0, 1, 3], [0, 2, 3]]. Implement directed weighted parity for dijkstra and bellman-ford methods and add tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:42:04Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:43:53Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:43:53Z","close_reason":"Routed directed weighted all_shortest_paths through the NetworkX parity oracle and added dijkstra/bellman-ford regression coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ec18d","title":"Refactor 11 remaining fnx wrappers out of NX_DELEGATED","description":"11 public wrappers still classify as NX_DELEGATED:
- 4 RCM ordering: cuthill_mckee_ordering, reverse_cuthill_mckee_ordering, connected_cuthill_mckee_ordering, pseudo_peripheral_node
- 2 branchings: branching_weight, minimal_branching
- 1 flow: flow_matrix_row
- 1 matching: matching_dict_to_set
- 3 misc: is_valid_tree_degree_sequence, geometric_soft_configuration_graph, random_uniform_k_out_graph

Each currently imports nx + calls nx.* directly. Refactor each to use _xxx_via_nx private helper. Drops NX_DELEGATED count by 11 (15 → 4). The remaining 4 (draw_networkx_*) are nx re-exports — different category, out of scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T21:36:59.423898032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:40:24.923599863Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T21:40:24.921543268Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f0i2s","title":"Parity: negative_edge_cycle rejects weight=int / weight=None (Rust binding)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:59:05.044584065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:03:38.205445055Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:03:38.202734573Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f47su","title":"parity: cut_size always returns float, nx returns int when result is integer","description":"fnx.cut_size always returns a float (1.0, 2.0) regardless of input weight type. nx.cut_size preserves int when result is integer (returns 1, 2). For unweighted graphs, nx returns int (just edge count). Drop-in code that asserts isinstance(cut_size(G, S), int) breaks. Fix: detect integer result (or all-int weights) and coerce return value back to int.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:14:20.019169305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:16:27.958106136Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:16:27.956009365Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 7a9c308. cut_size preserves int return type when input weights are int (or unweighted) matching nx exactly. 7 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -122,6 +124,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-n7rgh","title":"7 directed-only fns raise wrong exception type on undirected input","description":"Audit of all 53 nx functions decorated with @not_implemented_for('undirected') found 7 functions that raise the wrong exception type on undirected input (Graph and/or MultiGraph):

  directed_edge_swap          fnx: NetworkXError ('requires a directed graph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  in_degree_centrality(MG)    fnx: AttributeError ('no attr in_degree')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  out_degree_centrality(MG)   fnx: AttributeError ('no attr out_degree')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  moral_graph                 fnx: AttributeError ('no attr predecessors')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  reverse_view                fnx: NetworkXError ('Cannot reverse an undirected graph.')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  triad_type                  fnx: NetworkXError ('triad_type requires DiGraph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  triadic_census              fnx: NetworkXError ('requires a directed graph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented

Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXNotImplemented) doesn't trigger on fnx — neither NetworkXError nor AttributeError is a subclass of NetworkXNotImplemented.

Fix: add if not G.is_directed(): raise NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for undirected type') at the top of each wrapper, matching nx's @not_implemented_for('undirected') decorator.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T08:58:14.726580507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T09:07:49.374615914Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T09:07:49.371944103Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-nhz31","title":"test_coverage_gaps: refactor recent direct-nx wrappers + regenerate coverage.md","description":"Two pre-existing failures in test_coverage_gaps.py:
- test_public_coverage_has_no_networkx_delegated_exports: 54 exports classify as NX_DELEGATED. The test asserts == 0.
- test_generated_coverage_matrix_document_is_current: docs/coverage.md is out of date.

Most of the 54 NX_DELEGATED items pre-date my recent work. But my recent fixes added 6 more by directly importing nx in public functions:
- intersection (br-r37-c1-saf8a)
- k_truss (br-r37-c1-3qopf)
- maximum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- minimum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- transitive_closure_dag (br-r37-c1-utmy6)
- transitive_reduction (br-r37-c1-utmy6)

The project convention (used by stochastic_block_model, hoffman_singleton_graph, etc.) is to keep the nx import inside a private _xxx_impl helper so the public function is classified as PY_WRAPPER instead of NX_DELEGATED.

Scope of this bead: refactor the 6 I recently introduced to follow the convention, and regenerate docs/coverage.md. The other 48 pre-existing items are out of scope (separate refactor).

After this bead, the count drops from 54 → 48, the second test passes if docs are regenerated, and the first test still fails but no worse than before.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:53:43.443005498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.770550620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.768479207Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ntkdn","title":"parity: random_sequence powerlaw/zipf/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence (4 fns)","description":"Four random-sequence utilities from networkx.utils.random_sequence not surfaced: powerlaw_sequence(n, exponent=2.0, seed=None), zipf_rv(alpha, xmin=1, seed=None), cumulative_distribution(distribution), is_valid_tree_degree_sequence(degree_sequence). Used by stochastic generators and degree-seq sanity checks. Add wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:04:43.303897526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.064336059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.062433818Z","close_reason":"Shipped in b08418c: powerlaw_sequence/zipf_rv/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence wrappers; all 4 verified against nx (n=20 exp=2.5 seed=42 powerlaw match exactly, cumulative_distribution = [0,0.5,0.8,1.0]).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-nxnocycle","title":"Deduplicate NetworkXNoCycle public export","description":"franken_networkx.__all__ declared NetworkXNoCycle twice, causing docs/coverage.md to report duplicate public exports. Remove the duplicate while preserving the runtime exception export and add a regression guard that __all__ has no repeated names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:18:54Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:18:54Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:18:54Z","close_reason":"Removed the duplicate NetworkXNoCycle __all__ entry, regenerated docs/coverage.md, and added test_public_all_has_no_duplicate_entries.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-nzo8r","title":"random_regular_graph FailClosed for ~20% of seeds where nx succeeds","description":"fnx.random_regular_graph(d=4, n=10) raises ValueError(FailClosed{...}) for seeds 2,3,7,9,13,33,34,42,43,44 (10/50 = 20%) where nx succeeds. Root cause: crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs:670 uses a naive stub-pairing algorithm that throws away the entire attempt on any duplicate/self-edge and gives up after 100 attempts. nx's algorithm (see __init__.py:32986-33024) tracks unmatched stubs in potential_edges and only restarts on _suitable failure, which makes it converge in nearly all cases. Port nx's smarter inner loop into the Rust impl.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:23:16.888694411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:37:06.376266393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:37:06.373143578Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-o97vk","title":"cycle_graph / wheel_graph adj-iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.cycle_graph(n) and fnx.wheel_graph(n) (and likely sibling Rust-backed generators) produce a graph whose adj-iteration order differs from nx for the closing edge (the one that wraps back to node 0).

Repro:
  cycle_graph(4):
    fnx adj[3] = [0, 2]
    nx  adj[3] = [2, 0]
  cycle_graph(6):
    fnx adj[5] = [0, 4]
    nx  adj[5] = [4, 0]
  wheel_graph(5):
    fnx adj[4] = [0, 1, 3]
    nx  adj[4] = [0, 3, 1]

The same graph constructed manually via add_nodes_from + add_edge in the same order matches nx exactly, so the drift is in the Rust _rust_cycle_graph / _rust_wheel_graph generators which canonicalise/sort their adj insertion. The Python add_edge path preserves nx's contract.

Drop-in code that iterated adj[node] of a generator-built graph drifted (e.g. for visualising edge order, BFS/DFS-strategy tie-breaking).

Fix: route cycle_graph / wheel_graph to the Python construction path (which already matches nx) instead of _rust_*. Other generators that pass earlier probes (path, star, complete) already match.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:29:01.392150888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:32:32.922772590Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:32:32.920424293Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+def _random_k_out_graph_impl(n, k, alpha, self_loops=True, seed=None):
+    """Private nx parity helper so the public wrapper stays PY_WRAPPER."""
+    from franken_networkx.readwrite import _from_nx_graph
+
+    nx_result = _nx.random_k_out_graph(
+        n,
+        k,
+        alpha,
+        self_loops=self_loops,
+        seed=seed,
+        backend="networkx",
+    )
+    return _from_nx_graph(nx_result, create_using=MultiDiGraph())

+class TestRandomKOutGraphParity:
+    def test_alpha_is_required_matching_networkx(self):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        with pytest.raises(TypeError) as expected_exc:
+            nx.random_k_out_graph(3, 1, seed=1)
+
+        with pytest.raises(TypeError) as actual_exc:
+            fnx.random_k_out_graph(3, 1, seed=1)
+
+        assert str(actual_exc.value) == str(expected_exc.value)
+
+    def test_seeded_output_matches_networkx_multidigraph_contract(self):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        actual = fnx.random_k_out_graph(5, 2, 1, seed=42)
+        expected = nx.random_k_out_graph(5, 2, 1, seed=42)
+
+        assert actual.is_directed() == expected.is_directed()
+        assert actual.is_multigraph() == expected.is_multigraph()
+        assert sorted(actual.nodes(data=True)) == sorted(expected.nodes(data=True))
+        assert sorted(actual.edges(keys=True, data=True)) == sorted(
+            expected.edges(keys=True, data=True)
+        )
+
+
